title: HAProxy IngressClass and Controller
description:
## HAProxy IngressClass and Controller

When multiple ingress controllers run in the same cluster, Kubernetes uses the `IngressClass` resource to determine which controller should handle a given `Ingress` object.

How does a Kubernetes `Ingress` resource declare that it should be handled by the **HAProxy** ingress controller?

options.text: By setting the annotation `kubernetes.io/ingress.class: haproxy` or spec.ingressClassName: haproxy on the Ingress resource
By deploying the Ingress in the same namespace as the HAProxy controller pod
HAProxy automatically claims all Ingress resources in the cluster regardless of class
By setting a label `controller: haproxy` on the Ingress resource

explanation: An Ingress is bound to a specific controller via `spec.ingressClassName` (the modern approach, referencing an `IngressClass` object) or the legacy annotation `kubernetes.io/ingress.class`. HAProxy's Helm chart creates an IngressClass named `haproxy` by default. Without a class selector, the HAProxy controller ignores Ingress objects it wasn't told to manage. Namespace colocation and labels have no bearing on controller selection.

hints.title: Checking available IngressClasses
hints.body: Use kubectl to list IngressClass objects. The HAProxy one is typically named 'haproxy'.
